title: Graceful Venezuelan Fish Soup
description: A floral, springy fish soup that balances delicate fish stock with aromatic herbs and a touch of render‑fat, yet keeps it low‑fat and festive.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and add the cod liver oil until it melts and foams slightly.
Add the onion, garlic, and red bell pepper, sautéing until the vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook for another 2 minutes until they break down.
Pour in the fish stock and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then add the cumin, bay leaf, and a pinch of sea salt.
Allow the soup to simmer for 10 minutes so the flavors meld together.
Gently fold in the fish pieces, simmering for an additional 4-5 minutes until the fish is opaque and cooked through.
Remove the bay leaf, stir in the chopped cilantro stems, then discard them and add the fresh cilantro leaves.
Taste the soup and adjust seasoning with lime juice and a splash of orange zest if desired.
Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with a lime wedge and a sprinkle of extra cilantro.
